skill.name: slack-automation
skill.description: Automate Slack messaging, channel management, search, reactions, and threads via Rube MCP (Composio). Send messages, search conversations, manage channels/users, and react to messages programmatically.
skill.summary: Automate Slack workspace operations including messaging, search, channel management, and reaction workflows through Rube MCP integration with Composio's Slack toolkit.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) describing how to use Slack automation via Rube MCP (Composio). Static analyzer flagged 160 patterns, but all are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting for tool names, not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is a documented MCP endpoint. No executable code, cryptographic implementations, or reconnaissance functionality exists. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Automate Slack Messaging and Channel Management
content.value_statement: Managing Slack workflows manually consumes significant time. This skill enables programmatic message sending, search, channel management, and reactions through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Send and edit messages in channels and direct messages using markdown formatting
Search messages across workspace with filters for channel, user, and date ranges
List and retrieve information about channels, users, and user groups
Add, retrieve, and remove emoji reactions on messages
Manage threaded conversations and schedule messages for future delivery
Resolve channel names and user identifiers to their corresponding IDs
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Slack OAuth authentication
Cannot access private channels where the bot has not been invited
Search results may have delays for recently posted messages
Message scheduling is limited to 120 days in advance

use_cases.title: Team Communication Automation
use_cases.description: Automate daily standup reminders, project updates, and team announcements across multiple channels without manual posting.

use_cases.title: Conversation Search and Retrieval
use_cases.description: Quickly locate important decisions, links, or files shared in Slack conversations using targeted keyword and filter-based searches.

use_cases.title: Channel and User Management
use_cases.description: Audit workspace membership, enumerate channels, and manage user group memberships for access control workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Channel Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a message to the #general channel with the following content: [Your message here]. Use markdown formatting for readability.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Specific Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Slack for messages containing 'deployment' from @john.doe in the #engineering channel posted after 2024-01-01. Return the top 10 results sorted by relevance.

prompt_templates.title: Threaded Conversation Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the message with timestamp [ts] in channel [channel-id], fetch the complete thread, and post a reply that summarizes the discussion points.

prompt_templates.title: Workspace Audit Report
prompt_templates.prompt: List all public channels and users in the workspace. For each channel, retrieve member count and creation date. Compile a summary report of workspace activity.

output_examples.input: Send a welcome message to #new-hires channel
output_examples.output: Message successfully posted to #new-hires (C12345ABCDE). Message timestamp: 1708876543.210987. The message appears in the channel with proper markdown formatting applied.

output_examples.input: Search for messages containing 'API documentation' from last week
output_examples.output: Found 7 matching messages. Results include links to Confluence pages, GitHub repositories, and discussion threads about API changes. Thread expansions show 3 additional replies with code examples.

output_examples.input: List all users in the workspace
output_examples.output: Retrieved 45 workspace members including active users, admins, and bot accounts. Results include user IDs, display names, email addresses, and team affiliations. Pagination completed across 2 pages.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to retrieve current tool schemas before executing any Slack operations
Persist channel IDs and message timestamps from responses for subsequent edit or thread operations
Handle cursor-based pagination by following response_metadata.next_cursor until empty to retrieve complete result sets
anti_patterns: Do not send messages without first resolving channel names to channel IDs using SLACK_FIND_CHANNELS
Avoid omitting the thread_ts parameter when replying to messages, as this creates new top-level posts instead of threaded replies
Do not ignore HTTP 429 rate limit responses; always honor the Retry-After header before retrying list operations

faq.question: How do I connect my Slack workspace to this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'slack'. If not active, follow the returned auth link to complete OAuth. Confirm status shows ACTIVE before running workflows.

faq.question: Why can't I find a private channel using SLACK_FIND_CHANNELS?
faq.answer: Private channels require the bot to be invited before they appear in search results. Invite the bot to the private channel first, then retry the find operation.

faq.question: How do I mention users in messages?
faq.answer: Use the format <@USER_ID> where USER_ID is resolved via SLACK_FIND_USERS. The @username format does not work; you must use the numeric user ID.

faq.question: What is the difference between SLACK_LIST_ALL_CHANNELS and SLACK_LIST_CONVERSATIONS?
faq.answer: SLACK_LIST_ALL_CHANNELS returns only public channels. SLACK_LIST_CONVERSATIONS includes private channels and direct messages when you have appropriate permissions.

faq.question: How do I read threaded replies to a message?
faq.answer: SLACK_FETCH_CONVERSATION_HISTORY only returns the main channel timeline. Use SLACK_FETCH_MESSAGE_THREAD_FROM_A_CONVERSATION with the parent message's thread_ts parameter to retrieve threaded replies.

faq.question: Why am I getting 403 errors on some Slack operations?
faq.answer: Missing OAuth scopes can cause 403 errors. Check your app's permissions using SLACK_GET_APP_PERMISSION_SCOPES and ensure the required scopes are granted in your Slack app settings.
